output.final_deliverable:
Exploration path and results (evidence-limited)

1) Search (starting action)
- Query used (from merged results): "recent news article climate change \"climate change\" news 2025 2026 major outlet".
- Search result sample (merged results provided two candidate articles):
  - "See how climate change is accelerating - Washington Post"
    URL (as returned in merged results): https://www.washingtonpost.com/climate-environment/interactive/2026/climate-change-temperature-rate-
  - "Where things stand on climate change in 2026 » Yale Climate Connections"
    URL: https://yaleclimateconnections.org/2026/01/where-things-stand-on-climate-change-in-2026/

2) Selection / plan (from merged results)
- I selected the Washington Post interactive piece as the primary starting article (title and URL shown above) to follow its in-article links to find: (1) a scientific research paper or study, (2) a government policy document, and (3) an organization's position statement.

3) What I can (and cannot) provide from the merged results
- Available evidence: the search query and the two candidate article titles and URLs (shown above). These are the only concrete items provided in the merged results.
- Missing evidence: the merged results do NOT include the content of the Washington Post article (or the Yale article), nor any in-article links or references from that article. Therefore I do not have the URLs or text for any of the following that you requested:
    a) a scientific research paper/study cited by the chosen news article — MISSING
    b) a government policy document linked from the chosen news article — MISSING
    c) an organization’s position statement linked from the chosen news article — MISSING

4) Consequence and next steps (what would be done given full article content)
- Given the Washington Post article content or its list of links, I would: (a) open each in-article link, (b) identify and record the URL and bibliographic details of any scientific paper, government policy document, and organization statement; (c) summarize each item and explain how it relates to the news article. Those summaries would cite the exact URLs.

5) Summary of what I can deliver now (based only on available evidence)
- Starting news-article candidates found via search (2 URLs above).
- Chosen starting article: Washington Post interactive (URL above).
- Missing: all in-article links and the three requested target documents (scientific study, government policy document, organization position statement). These items are required to complete the task but are not present in the merged results you provided.

If you want me to continue now, please provide either: (A) the full URL of the Washington Post article (or the article text) so I can follow its links, or (B) permission to perform a live visit of the Washington Post or another chosen article so I can extract and summarize the linked study, policy document, and organization statement.
output.action_summary: I searched for recent news articles about climate change and found two candidate articles (Washington Post interactive and Yale Climate Connections). I selected the Washington Post piece as the starting article (URL shown). However, the merged results do not include the article content or any in-article links, so I cannot locate or summarize the requested scientific paper, government policy document, or organization position statement. To complete the task I need the article content or permission/ability to visit the article URL so I can follow its links and extract the three items.

merged_results.title: Search for a recent news article about climate change (find candidate articles and their URLs)

result.query: recent news article climate change "climate change" news 2025 2026 major outlet
result.intent: Find 4–6 recent, reputable news articles (BBC, The Guardian, NYTimes, Washington Post, Reuters, AP) about climate change that include in-article links or references to external studies, government policy documents, or organization position statements.
vector_context: Select best news article and plan link-following (analysis of search results to choose one article to visit)

results.title: See how climate change is accelerating - Washington Post
results.description: According to a Washington Post analysis, the fastest warming rate on record occurred in the last 30 years. The Post used a dataset from NASA to analyze global average surface temperatures from 1880 to 2025.

results.title: Where things stand on climate change in 2026 » Yale Climate Connections
results.description: And U.S. emissions reversed their long-term downward trend to instead increase in 2025. But despite the bleak domestic picture, the rest of the world made significant climate and clean energy progress. China continued to emerge as a clean technology leader, positioning itself to overtake the U.S. as the next global economic superpower. While these trends seem likely to continue in 2026, the U.S. Congress has the opportunity to pass major climate and clean energy legislation in the coming year – unless the Trump administration derails it.

results.title: Climate News -- ScienceDaily
results.description: 7, 2026  Scientists have discovered that wildfires release far more air-polluting gases than previously estimated. Many of these hidden emissions can transform into fine particles that are dangerous to ... Scientists Found a Dangerous Feedback Loop Accelerating Arctic Warming · Dec. 29, 2025  The Arctic is changing rapidly, and scientists have uncovered a powerful mix of natural and human-driven processes fueling that change.

results.title: Five Things to Know About Climate Change in 2025 | Climate Central
results.description: As of fall 2025, the U.S. has rolled back landmark 2021-2022 policies to boost clean energy and climate resilience, and is set to withdraw from the Paris Agreement by January 2026.

results.title: Year in Review: The Biggest Climate Headlines of 2025 | Earth.Org
results.description: Under pressure from major petrostates, including Saudi Arabia and Russia, COP30 President André Corrêa do Lago ultimately announced a compromise: a voluntary “roadmap” for transitioning away from fossil fuels.

results.title: Climate : NPR
results.description: January 14, 2026  Scientists calculate that last year was one of the three hottest on record, along with 2024 and 2023. The trend indicates that warming could be speeding up, climate monitoring teams reported. Wildfire smoke towered over burned houses from the Palisades Fire on Jan. 10, 2025.

details.intent: From the search results (node 1), choose the single best news article to start from. Criteria: recency, reputable outlet, and presence of outbound links to at least one scientific study, one government policy document, and one organization position statement — or at least clearly-cited references that can be followed.
target_facts: Reason for selection (which of the three required link types are present or likely to be reachable)
Planned order of following links (which link to visit first, second, third) and fallback criteria if a needed link type is not present in the article
details.extraction_plan: After node 1 returns, inspect the snippets and select the article that best matches criteria. If multiple articles look suitable, prefer the one that explicitly links to external content (e.g., DOI link, gov.uk or .gov links, or links to organizational pages). If the article lacks one of the three types, plan to follow quoted sources or references in the article to find the missing type.
details.rationale: We must choose one starting article from the search results. This think step will produce the exact URL to visit next and a clear plan for following links in order to reduce uncertainty and ensure we can find the three required document types.

details.query: recent news article climate change "climate change" news 2025 2026 major outlet
details.intent: Find 4–6 recent, reputable news articles (BBC, The Guardian, NYTimes, Washington Post, Reuters, AP) about climate change that include in-article links or references to external studies, government policy documents, or organization position statements.
target_facts: snippets that show links or references (in-article links, cited studies, or policy docs)
list of outbound links shown in snippet (if any) or indication that article contains external references
verification_steps: Prefer articles published within the last 18 months (2024–2026); if none found, accept most recent available.

Check snippets for explicit external links or named studies (e.g., DOI, journal names), government agencies (e.g., EPA, Department of Energy, Climate Ministry), or organizations (e.g., IPCC, WWF, Sierra Club).
Return at least one article that clearly includes at least one external link to a study, policy document, or organizational statement.
details.extraction_plan: Return search result snippets including exact result URLs for the top 6 matches. For each snippet include the visible snippet text and any explicit link targets shown in the snippet. Prioritize items from major outlets and those that mention studies/policies/organizations.
